text:
Title: A bit too tiny sponges with handle for applying liquid foundation or cream blush
Review: There are 12 small round with tear drop tip foam sponges with ribbon handles in this package with one plastic carry case for the used sponge. They are a bit too tiny in size as the handle can only be slipped over two of my fingers. Nevertheless, this works well to apply cream foundation or for blending cream rouge (cheek creams/blush). The current price of $9.99 comes to a pricey $0.83 cents per sponge. If these were a little bigger I would have rated them 4 stars.
cot: 1. Clues: 'a bit too tiny', 'only be slipped over two of my fingers', 'pricey' vs. 'works well', 'If these were a little bigger I would have rated them 4 stars'. 2. Reasoning: The size limitation and price are notable drawbacks, but functionality is confirmed. The explicit counterfactual higher rating indicates the product is close to satisfactory. 'Nevertheless' signals acceptance despite limitations. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with near-miss satisfaction.

text:
Title: Great case/cover keypad for IPad Air
Review: Not sure why all the negative reviews on this Logitech item. All I can think of is that there were problems initially with the cover which Logitech corrected in a later release. Anyway, I love this cover. I find it protects my IPad Air oerfectly and looks sharp and sleek. The keyboard itself is neatly inside and, when opened, allows for a feeling that you are typing on a traditional full size keyboard. Although not inexpensive by any means, it is a great tool to use particularly when doing a lot of typing (I hate using the keyboard on the screen). I highly recommend this to everyone. Like I said, I could find no fault with the cover/case an it performs as it should.
cot: 1. Clues: 'love this cover', 'protects perfectly', 'looks sharp and sleek', 'great tool', 'highly recommend', 'no fault' - overwhelmingly positive language. 2. Reasoning: The reviewer enthusiastically defends the product against negative reviews, citing multiple specific benefits. Despite acknowledging high price, they find value. Strong recommendation language and emotional attachment ('love') indicate high satisfaction. 3. Verdict: Positive sentiment aligns with 4-star rating - strong approval with minor reservation about cost.

text:
Title: Too Strong of Fragrance
Review: I was looking for a salt scrub to use on my hands.  I have tried several different salt and sugar scrubs over the years. I've classified them in to two different types. There are the dry ones and wet ones. This is one of the wet ones. When you scoop it out, it's dense and a bit solid, like Crisco. The amount of salt is decent and allows for adequate sloughing. But the consistency is greasier than what I wanted. After I rub it in to my hands, I rinse it off with warm to hot water. Even after I had removed all of the product, I had to keep rinsing because it leaves greasiness behind. I was satisfied with how the product left my hands feeling though. I was also disappointed with the fragrance. I was expecting a more cherry/fruity scent with a bit of floral to it. Instead it's more of a flowery perfume and a bit too strong. I love a strong fragrance, but not this one. It isn't a natural scent. The price was good, but not what I was wanting.
cot: 1. Clues: Positive: 'decent' salt, 'adequate sloughing', 'satisfied with how the product left my hands feeling'. Negative: 'greasier than what I wanted', 'leaves greasiness behind', 'disappointed with the fragrance', truncated ending suggests ongoing complaint. 2. Reasoning: Functional effectiveness (exfoliation, final hand feel) is balanced against undesired properties (greasy residue, disappointing scent). The detailed neutral classification of product type and methodical testing suggest careful evaluation rather than strong reaction. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment due to mixed functional results and unmet expectations.

However, I will say that this Dove deodorant did apply easily, is beautifully designed and as I mentioned earlier has an amazing fresh scent. I know there are people out there that love this type of rollon, but since I often like to go sleeveless, I prefer a product that you can not see if you raise your arms. This is a great protector, though, as I wore it while playing tennis and I did feel fresh and clean when I was done (just as well if not better than any gel deodorant I may use.

The only reason I tried this one, was because it was recommended to me by a friend w some of the same issues I have.
I have severe back, neck & hip issues, with severe pain & can't get into a shower or bath anymore.
If I could do all the bending & twisting needed, to spray this stuff on & then wipe off, I don't know why I need a product like this in the first place, lol.
It smelled alright, but left my arms & legs sticky, even after wiping off with paper or cotton towels.
It did eventually dry, but it just isn't very helpful for me.

Title: DirectTV Now all but unusable on Fire TV Cube
Review: I've been trying to use the cube with DTVNow for a few months now and have reset to factory defaults, deinstalled app multiple times and still have the same issue, which is the video and audio will stutter when starting the app after being dormant overnight or idle for several hours.  If I restart the Fire Cube, the app will work ok.  It's never been as fast the Gen 2 FireTV box we had but I was willing to deal with that for the slick device control the Cube offered. Not being able to use my primary  TV streaming service is a show stopper. Shame as it could have been a great device, I suspect it's a processor and/or lack of memory issue, but not certain. Will be selling it CHEAP and replacing with with a ROKU or Invidia Shield.
cot: 1. Clues: 'all but unusable', 'show stopper', 'Shame', 'selling it CHEAP', 'replacing'. Mild positive: 'slick device control', 'could have been a great device'. 2. Review: The print and color is just like the pictures. I washed it in gentle cycle inside a laundry bag,  dried it, removed it from dryer immediately upon completion... so so so many wrinkles and the color fads after one wash. I love the color and style. However, the ponytail pouch is shaped weirdly. If I roll my hair in a bun, I can't get it into the ponytail pouch.  It took some effort to push my hair in little by little. The entry area is stretchy but small. I wore it to work today; had to constantly reposition the cap. It will not stay in place, keeps on slipping back. My hair inside the pouch does not stay in place either. I had to retuck it in 2x over an 8 hr shift. I find it inconvenient. I don't like to touch or expose my hair at work because I'm always caring for sick patients. This cap defeats the purpose of protection. Such a waste of money.
cot: 1. Clues: Title is lukewarm; initial positive on appearance; extensive list of functional failures (wrinkles, fading, weird shape, poor fit, constant repositioning, hair falling out); explicit 'inconvenient' and 'don't like'; truncated at end. 2. Reasoning: Despite 'love the color and style,' the operational failures are numerous and severe, making product unusable for intended purpose. The 1-star rating overrides the single aesthetic positive. 3. Verdict: Strongly negative sentiment—functional failures dominate aesthetic appeal.

text:

It does not leave any stickiness.

text:
Title: Really cleanses / strips my hair; I would not use this more than once per week
Review: First of all I do not like the tin bottle because it marks up my acrylic tub with gray stains. Second, I hate having to unscrew a cap to get to the shampoo. It does pour out easily. The creamy white shampoo lathers pretty well and really cleanses. However, it feels like it is stripping my  hair. I will only use this when my hair needs a good cleansing. At the current price of $22, I would never buy this. The bottle it comes in and the detergent-like cleanse is just too much to handle.
cot: 1. Clues: Multiple negatives: packaging damages surfaces, inconvenient cap design, stripping effect on hair, poor value at $22 price point, would never repurchase. Limited positives: easy pouring, good lather, effective cleansing (though too strong). 2. Reasoning: While the product technically functions (cleanses, lathers), the execution is deeply flawed—damaging packaging, overly harsh formula, and terrible value. The reviewer explicitly states 'never buy this' and 'too much to handle,' indicating strong negative sentiment despite acknowledging some functional effectiveness. The 2-star rating reflects that the product works but is unpleasant to use. 3. Verdict: Negative sentiment with minor functional acknowledgments.

text:
Title: Nice large suitcase, basic functionality, lightweight but high priced
Review: Nothing much more to add to other reviews here. It took me a while to even figure out how to open this suitcase (despite the fact that the lock was set to 0000. However, after about 15 minutes of working on it, I finally realized there was a button to slide. Inside there are two large pocket areas and the rest is open (with secure straps to keep your clothing in place). It is a 29" size, prides itself on being large. I found it to be adequate when compared to my Travelpro luggage (I think they have far more pockets, even small ones where I can put toiletries which this does not have). The biggest plus is the fact that it is lightweight (so I know I will be able to zip through the airport). Wheels are multi-directional and it is easy to maneuver. Biggest issue to me is the price, but I know there are people out there who love the designer products but over $500 for this suitcase is too high. I haven't tried to reset the lock yet. I see there are some videos out there on You Tube so I will play around with that later.
cot: 1. Clues: Mixed elements - usability issue (difficulty opening), comparison showing inferior features to competitor, but strong positive on weight ('biggest plus', 'zip through the airport'). Title notes 'high priced' and 'basic functionality'. 2. Reasoning: The 4-star rating reflects that benefits (lightweight, adequate size) outweigh drawbacks (learning curve, fewer pockets). The enthusiastic praise for weight suggests genuine satisfaction with core travel function. 3. Verdict: Positive with reservations, not neutral - the practical advantage of lightweight design drives overall approval.

text:
Title: Functional blush brush w/no bristle shedding; would have preferred longer bristles & heavier handle Review: This is a basic angled blush brush. It works well for applying powder blush. The brush feels great in my hands--although I do wish it had a silicone grip on it and a heavier handle. The bristles do not shed and the head provides decent coverage. I do wish the bristles were just a little bit longer and that the bristles were a little softer. In general, this is a basic but functional foundation brush.

The current price of $18.99 for the quality of this brush is about $10 overpriced, in my opinion.I would have expected a higher quality handle and much softer bristles at that price.
cot: 1. Clues: Functional performance with no shedding and decent coverage; criticisms regarding price being significantly overvalued and desire for longer bristles and heavier handle. 2. Reasoning: The reviewer acknowledges the brush performs its basic function adequately with positive aspects like no bristle shedding and decent coverage. However, the strong emphasis on the product being overpriced by a significant margin, combined with specific feature disappointments, creates a balanced view where the utility is recognized but value is questioned. This equilibrium between functional satisfaction and value disappointment supports a middle-ground classification. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment reflecting mixed satisfaction with product functionality versus price and feature expectations.

text:

text:
Title: so I could handle that because they're all pretty. But

It smells horrible of musty, moldy, have aired out since I received it with scented dryer sheets and hanging outside. Still smells. So, it was too late to return, I liked the purse so I purchased it in the original green one. Thank goodness this one doesn't smell. You just don't know what you're getting.

text:
Title: Wonderfully fragrant soap that cleans & lathers well but avoid washing certain areas; pricey Review: There is a warning on this package that this is not to be applied on eyes, ears, mouth, and genitals but the Amazon site says this is good for jock itch. Then it says if "wipes" contact these areas, rinse promptly and thoroughly with water. Also, the box says to apply the soap to the skin and allow it to stay on the skin for several minutes. With all these warnings, I would never order this again. Nevertheless, I love this soap.

This bar has tea tree oil in it, which gives it its antifungal properties. However,  it does not smell medicinal like tea tree oil. It smells wonderful. It cleans and lathers well also.  The current price of $8.99 is pricey for this small bar.
cot: 1. Clues: Positive elements include 'wonderfully fragrant,' 'smells wonderful,' 'cleans and lathers well,' 'I love this soap.' Negative elements include confusing warnings, 'would never order this again,' and 'pricey.' 2. Reasoning: The review contains strong positive sentiment about the product's actual performance and scent, but significant concerns about safety warnings and price. The 3-star rating reflects this genuine ambivalence—love for the product itself but frustration with warnings and cost. 3. Verdict: The mixed positive and negative elements balance out to a neutral overall sentiment matching the 3-star rating.

So I returned this.

I was very disappointed.

text:
Title: Tight Cuff Band Requires Skinny Legs Review: I love the way these K. BELL WOMEN’S SOFT AND DREAMY CREW SOCKS (3 Pack, size 9-11) feel on my feet, but I don’t have the skinny legs you need to wear them. The socks go about halfway up my legs, and the top cuff band is far too tight for comfort. I had to fold the socks down to make anklets, to be able to wear them at all. (I’m NOT overweight, but have sort of fat calves—12-1/2” around at the point where the sock cuffs hit.)

The 3-pack consists of one pair of navy socks with dots (very cute), and two pairs of jet black socks. They’re easy care socks—machine wash cold, no chlorine bleach, tumble dry low.

Aside from the circulation-strangling cuffs, this 3-pack contains no fewer than 6 of those horrid clear nylon hangtag-attachers that need to be cut off so carefully. It’s a challenge to NOT cut the sock fabric, and to keep from dropping a piece of the attacher on the floor (where bare feet will surely find it, painfully, if you can’t see where it dropped).
cot: 1. Clues: 'love the way...feel on my feet', 'very cute' are positive; 'far too tight', 'circulation-strangling', 'horrid' are strongly negative; workaround required to use product. 2. Reasoning: Fundamental design flaw (cuff tightness) severely impacts usability despite excellent foot comfort and appearance. Detailed physical description and workaround indicate significant but not total dissatisfaction. 3-star rating captures 'mixed with lean negative.' 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with significant negative element.

text:
Title: Nicely scented but the this face wash is more of a gel and does not produce a lather Review: I love trying, using, and purchasing men's products because I tend to like them more. This face wash has a nice fragrance but I do not like it because it has a thick viscosity like a gel that produces absolutely no lather. I just do not feel like I cleaned my face after using this.

On the other hand, I love their [[ASIN:B0748G11ZG face moisturizer]].

UPDATE October 30, 2017: My boyfriend tried this and did not care that the soap us thick. He said it does not lather well but it cleans well. He rates this 2 stars because he does not see the point of this for a man. He says it is another step in a busy day when he can just wash his face as part of a shower.
cot: 1. Clues: Personal negative experience (no lather, unclean feeling) balanced against partner's milder assessment, brand loyalty to other product, boyfriend's 2-star rating with different reasoning, no lather as core issue. 2. Reasoning: The 3-star rating synthesizes these perspectives—reviewer's dissatisfaction with format, partner's functional acceptance but practical objection. The update complicates but doesn't resolve tension; both find product suboptimal for different reasons. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with moderate confidence, reflecting genuine uncertainty about product value.

text:
Title: Nice shampoo/conditioner combo; don’t like that bottles look like juice drinks Review: This is a nice shampoo and conditioner set. The shampoo lathers pretty good but not excellent. The conditioner leaves my hair nourished and tame. I like the fragrance when I smell it in the bottle but during use it smells different and not as pleasant.

What I do not like is that the bottles look like juice drinks. I can see someone making a mistake and thinking this is a food item.

In summary, I like the results on my short, fine curly hair but not enough to order this again. It would have to smell more fragrant and the shampoo would have to lather better.  I personally prefer the TRESemmé Keratin Smooth combo more.
cot: 1. Clues: Positive: 'nice', 'nourished and tame', 'like the results'. Negative: 'not excellent', 'not as pleasant', 'don't like', 'not enough to order this again', 'prefer...more'. 2. Reasoning: Mixed review with clear positives (hair results) and negatives (scent inconsistency, packaging safety concern, inferior to competitor). The reviewer explicitly states they would not repurchase, indicating lukewarm satisfaction matching the 3-star rating. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with balanced pros and cons.

The spandex does NOT offer firm control—what control there is, is barely there, and the smoothing or slimming effect is minimal.  However, it’s a nice panty.

text:
Title: Similar to a regular dish sponge except it feels like gel; hanging loop is a thread; too pricey Review: This is mildly exfoliating until its fully wet. It's really just a sponge that feels almost similar to a regular Ocelo kitchen sponge. The only difference is that the consistency feels more rubbery or "gel-like." This holds a lot of water but it does not lather any better than a regular sponge. What I absolutely hate is the cheap thread going through the sponge that one can use to hang it. The thread is so cheap, that it reminds me of sewing thread and will definitely break easily.

The current price of $13.25 is outrageous for this sponge in my opinion. I personally do not like anything but the color. After having tried this, it will be repuprosed as a not-so-good kitchen sponge.

UPDATE 5/11/2021 - I threw this sponge out two days ago. I felt like I was washing with jelly. It has a disgusting feel to me that I just could not use this anymore. I tried using this as a dish sponge but I also could not stand it for that.
cot: 1. Clues: 'mildly exfoliating', 'holds a lot of water', 'color' (weak positive); 'really just', 'only difference', 'does not lather any better', 'absolutely hate', 'cheap', 'definitely break easily', 'outrageous', 'do not like anything but', 'not-so-good' (strongly negative). 2. Reasoning: Systematic devaluation through comparison to common alternative (kitchen sponge), eliminating perceived premium value. The 'absolutely hate' and 'outrageous' language conveys strong negative affect. The planned repurposing as 'not-so-good' kitchen item indicates complete functional rejection. Price-value outrage is central grievance. Single aesthetic appreciation ('color') is explicitly isolated and insufficient. 3. text:
Title: Sheer luxury for your face!
Review: This is one of the best skin cremes I have ever used. Yes, it is expensive. But this is one of the times I can honestly say you get what you pay for. A little goes a long way, this should last you for months, so it is well worth it. Goes on rich and luxurious, absorbs super quick into your skin. I use it immediately after I shower to lock in moisture and I can say for the few months I have been using it now, my skin looks and feels fresher and softer. I love the fragrance and the way it applies. I can highly recommend this IS Clinitcal creme to anyone with 40+ skin. Minimizes wrinkles, adds moisture back to aging skin and makes your skin glow and feel wonderful. Takes years off your face!
cot: 1. Clues: Superlative positives: 'one of the best,' 'you get what you pay for,' 'well worth it,' 'rich and luxurious,' 'super quick,' 'fresher and softer,' 'love,' 'highly recommend,' specific benefits for target demographic. Acknowledged negative: 'expensive' immediately reframed as justified. 2. Reasoning: Exceptional satisfaction with comprehensive benefits across texture, efficacy, longevity, and results. The price acceptance demonstrates perceived value. Detailed usage pattern and timeframe confirm sustained positive experience. Strong recommendation with demographic specificity indicates confidence. 3. Verdict: Clearly positive matching the 5-star rating.

text:
Title: Way overpackaged for a gift; decent lather & cleansing wash w/subtle fragrance Review: My first impression of this set was not good. It comes in a box three times the size of the product with a lot of black paper padding. If a man were receiving this as a gift, I think he would be expecting more product and be a bit disappointed. There are two body washes and two beard/hair washes in this box. If I removed all the packaging I could fit three sets in this box.

The washes and hair washes are all the same. The fragrance is subtle. I used the hair wash on my hair and it really cleans with moderate lather. The same with the body wash. The results of both items do not blow me away. The washes are just okay but the packaging is a big negative. Also, the current price of $16.99 is a bit much for this ensemble. At this price, it comes to $4.25 per travel-sized bottle.
cot: 1. Clues: 'not good' first impression, 'disappointed', 'do not blow me away', 'just okay', 'big negative' vs 'really cleans', 'moderate lather'. 2. Reasoning: The 3-star rating reflects mediocrity — adequate performance overshadowed by excessive packaging and underwhelming results. The product works but fails to impress or justify price/presentation. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment matching the 3-star rating.

text:
Title: Not for Coolpix S3500
Review: Have to give it three stars because I honestly can't say how it is. Title: Silicone head too wobbly for scrubbing; needs 1 AA battery; vibration not enough Review: I am disappointed in this sonic facial cleaner. One AA battery is needed (NOT INCLUDED). I just pressed the center button and a tiny light (see photo) and a continuous but subtle vibration begins. The button can be pressed again for a change in vibration and again for what feels like a stop / start vibration.

I do find this difficult to use because the arm and head bend easily, so it is almost impossible to get any scrubbing action since the wand does not stay tight to the face.

The "mirror-looking" side is supposed to be used to gently massage wrinkle-prone skin but I have no interest in doing that. It is cooling though.

Unfortunately, I do not care for this facial cleansing brush. The arm needs to be stiffer to hold the brush head against my face. Also, the current price of $24.99 is ridiculous for this. Even if it were an excellent brush, I feel anything more than $10 is too much.

Instead of a 'regular' lipstick/lip gloss, this Nivea aid keeps my lips soft and smooth at all times and gives me the added benefit of being like a lip gloss. I highly recommend this to anyone with dry, chapped lips or anyone who just wants to look their best (while doing good for skin).

Title: A decent portable wireless keyboard choice Review: This is a decent wireless keyboard and a good choice for the price.

Pros:
* Spacing between keys is fairly standard (don't need to scrunch your fingers together much like some mini-keyboards)
* Connection to the computer is reliable
* Response time is good.
Cons:
* Right shift key is in a nonstandard location and too small
* Key action feel is a bit springy
* Key action travel is a bit too far which sometimes results in hitting two keys instead of one (more typing errors)
* Trackpad could be larger in area and the buttons moved down to make better use of available space (it would have made more sense for the buttons to be lined up with the bottom row of the keyboard; instead they are halfway between the 2nd and 3rd rows).

But all-in-all a solid choice for a portable wireless keyboard for casual use.
cot: 1. Clues: 'decent', 'good choice for the price' vs multiple cons about key placement, feel, travel, trackpad. Balanced pros/cons structure. 2. Reasoning: The review presents equal consideration of benefits and drawbacks. 'Decent' and 'good choice for the price' indicate acceptable value without excellence. My grown son has a average beard he usually shaves everyday. He likes a clean shave.
He has not used this in the shower, although its water proof, and can be use in the shower safely.
What he finds is, he has to use this Slow to get the close shave he likes. Using it fast he is not happy with the results.
This Braun razor does not pull his beard out while shaving, which his other More expensive razor does.

This is a nice middle of the road razor.
Taking off 1 1/2 stars for not having a trimer on this.

Title: Beautiful Fabric, Wide Brim, Shallow Crown Review: This TOMMY HILFIGER MEN’S CHAMBRAY MINI FLAG BASEBALL DAD CAP is constructed of beautiful chambray fabric (white weft, colored warp) that looks like mottled, faded blue denim. It’s a practical color that won’t easily show dirt. The cap’s bill is wide and long, to properly shade the eyes.

Unfortunately, the crown is shallow, so that the cap perches on the top of the head. (I think this is probably a “medium profile” cap, though it could be a “low profile” cap.) I find the shallow crown style to be both uncomfortable and unflattering. No amount of adjustment of the strap in back can fix this.

The back strap adjusts with a flat metal clasp. The strap end fits into a buttonhole that holds the end down and out of sight.

Title: Canon PIXMA MG2920

I always update my reviews if something useful arises.

text:
Title: Lightly fragrant bath bombs with surprise jewelry inside; decent performance but staining Review: [[VIDEOID:9faeeb655f0c302a58df3fb857db320a]] At the current price of $23.99, these bombs come to $4 each which is slightly expensive. Each bomb has a surprise stretchy bracelet inside that is encased in a plastic bubble.

I tried the rose bath bomb in my foot bath. The fragrance is nice but subtle. The suds lasted only eight minutes but the fragrance lasted the entire bath. The bomb dissolved to release the surprise in about five minutes. The color of the water was a cranberry red and the color stained my feet and towels but not my foot bath.

In summary, the performance of these bombs is decent and the suds are not as long lasting as I would like. The colored water stained--at least on the bomb I tried. The fragrance, however, is subtle but very nice. A young girl will love the surprise bracelets and acceptable performance of these bath bombs. However, at $4 each one--there are six of them, this is expensive. The six bombs are lavender, rose, lemongrass, jasmine, chamomile, and rosemary.
cot: 1. Clues: 'slightly expensive', 'suds lasted only eight minutes' (disappointing duration), 'stained my feet and towels' (significant negative), 'nice but subtle' fragrance (mild positive), 'decent' (neutral descriptor), jewelry surprise (novelty positive). 2. Reasoning: Mixed experience with notable flaws (staining, short suds, price) against mild pleasures (fragrance, surprise). The 'decent' summary and 3-star rating reflect adequate but unremarkable performance with inconveniences. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with balanced pros and cons.

text:
Title: Expensive facial toner that left my face slightly less acne-prone Review: I always shower first before using any products so that means my face was already clean. I then applied this religiously both day and night for one week before my moisturizing regimen. As instructed, I saturated one pad and wiped down my face being sure to avoid my eyes. Each pad was as clean as when I started. On a positive note, I did not have any allergic reaction to the salicyclic acid as I do with Proactiv's other products that contain Benzoyl peroxide. The only other benefit I noticed is that my face is a little less prone to acne. Other than this, this toner is not impressive enough for me to buy it at the outrageous price of $44.95.

Ingredients in order are:
- water
- butylene glycol
- hamamelis virginiana (witch hazel) water
- glycolic acid
- salicyclic acid
- sodium hydroxide
- portulaca oleracea extract
- polysorbate 20
- ethylhexylglycerin
- disodium EDTA
- sodium benzoate
- phenoxyethanol
cot: 1. Clues: Positives include no allergic reaction and slightly less acne. Negatives include no visible cleaning effect, 'not impressive', and 'outrageous price'. 2. Reasoning: The review presents balanced but underwhelming experience. Benefits are minimal (slight acne reduction) while drawbacks are significant (high price, no visible cleaning). The 3-star rating reflects this ambivalence—product works marginally but fails to justify cost or expectations. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment as the reviewer finds limited value but acknowledges some minor benefits.

text:
Title: It's probably just me
Review: Rating this printer as a 4 star for the following reason: First, print quality and speed are terrific. Scanning is done with ease and perfectly passes the data to my computer (where I e-mailed the scan to a friend). For the money, this printer is a great deal. Title: Lightly fragrant; produces rainbow-colored suds; color adheres; no face on bomb; $$$ Review: There is one super large individually wrapped bath bomb in this package. Based on the Amazon picture, I expected to have a face on the bomb or the bomb plastic and that is not the case. It is just an XL white cloud bath bomb.

It has a nice light fragrance when dry and in the bath. The ingredients are sodium bicarbonate, citric acid, aqua, fragrance, PVP, and benzyl benzoate.

It performs well, produces a lovely rainbow of suds, and dissolves in about 10-15 minutes. Since I personally do not like the color rings caused by bath bombs in my bath, I use them in a foot bath. When I was done, some color did adhere to my foot bath and my drying towel. I suspect if I used this in a bathtub I would not have had the same problem due to the water disbursement.

The current cost of $8.49 ($9.99 minus 15%) for one bomb is too expensive for this in my opinion especially when there is no face on the bomb and not even on the plastic.

However, the main item is the brush/mirror combo. The mirror actually acts like the handle of the brush. First of all, using the mirror as the handle is awkward. Also, once closed, it wobbles on the counter.

Title: I bought it for the gimmick and I was still disappointed Review: Don't get me wrong, it's a mouse. It functions like a mouse should and nothing else.

Pros:
-The tank-track scroll wheel is a neat gimmick to show your friends once. Whole reason I bought it basically.
-projected logo is cool, albeit blurry
-Feels ok in the hand, and the clicking action is satisfying (should expect as much from cherry)

Cons:
-Horrible, and I mean Horrible side button placement. They are way too far back, feel cheap and squishy, and hitting the back button requires you to be a hand gymnast. Either they tested this with child hands or didn't test it at all.
-Useless RGB. Seriously, it's nearly invisible, why even include it? I had it for a week before I realized it had RGB lighting
-sensitivity controls are a goldilocks game. Low is incredibly low, high makes your mouse a crackhead, and medium is manageable.
-super cheap packaging.

If you want to try a neat gimmick mouse, give it a shot. Don't expect it to be a great mouse though
cot: 1. Clues: 'still disappointed' in title despite knowing it's a gimmick; balanced pros/cons structure; functional but flawed; 'Horrible' emphasized for side buttons; 'Useless RGB.' 2. Reasoning: The reviewer's expectations were low (gimmick purchase) yet still unmet. The product works as a basic mouse but fails on promised features. Equal weight of pros and cons, with the cons being usability-impacting but not product-failing. The 3-star 'meh' sentiment fits this lukewarm assessment. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment—neither satisfied nor angry, just disappointed.

Title: Super dark long-lasting lipstick that glides on smoothly; smells like a Crayola crayon Review: This review is for the lipstick color labeled "watch me" which is a very deep burgundy color that is almost black.

This lipstick glides smoothly on my lips and stays put, even after eating. I do not wear lipstick for 24 hours, so I cannot attest to it's wearability for that length of time; however, I could still see the color after eight hours. Although, this is a matte finish, it still looks great. In the future, I will be applying another lipstick over this because the color is a bit deep for my fair complexion. 

What I find amazing about this lipstick is that when I blot my lips, very little of the color can be seen on the tissue. What I don't like is the Crayola crayon smell and taste of the lipstick and the fact that it is a bit too dark for me. Nevertheless, this is a great long lasting lipstick as long as one wants a muted matte finish and the color complements their complexion.

Title: Low-end brush set that does the job; it is a selection for every hair length Review: There are five brushes in this brush set that are STUFFED into a plastic zippered bag. Four of the brushes are radial brushes and one is a paddle / detangle type brush.

The radial brushes are good for blow drying my hair. Since there are several circumferences, there is a brush for almost every hair length. These are lightweight and the ribbed grip on the handle is excellent. I do not like that there is this metal blank name tag on the handle that breaks up the grip. I do not understand the purpose of that. The bristles are so, so on these brushes and made of hard nylon. They work and go through my hair easily but they will not keep their shape long.

This is a basic low-end functional set. At the current price of $25.99, it comes to $5.20 per brush.

Title: I love the pink color of this brush but I like URTHEONE brushes better; quick assembly required Review: I already own these [[ASIN:B08BNHZ833URTHEONE detangling brushes]] and love them. Therefore, I wanted a pink one like this for my pink bathroom so I ordered this.

There is one pink detangling brush with one rod that needs to be installed on top. I prefer a wider brush, so I installed it on the lower rung. 

I use my other brushes every other day to detangle my hair in the shower and love them but this pink one is not as great. First of all the handle is cheap and does not have a silicone grip. This means the grip is not as good. Second, the rod that gets installed only has two nubs on it that connect with the tines of the brush instead of several like the other brushes. This means it does not stay put as easily. However, it still works on detangling my short to medium curly hair in the shower and if some hair gets caught, it easily rinses out. In addition, this can be used as a regular brush too. 

The current price of $5.99 is reasonable for one brush but I recommend the other brand over this one.

text:
Title: Joker Smile
Review: I am so surprised at how much I dislike this lipstick. Liquid matte lipstick is my usual go-to. And Nyx is one of the brands I reach for again and again. But I really hate this formula. You get tons of color payoff. I didn’t find it particularly patchy and when I first put it on, I was pretty excited and thought it would be love. However, it was pretty sticky. And then, when it wears off, the mess is completely unbearable! It shifts and slides as it comes off. Meaning it ends up all over your face. At first, I tried to just wipe it with a paper towel. Nope. Just smeared it more. So, I tried a wet paper towel. Nope. At this point, I’m looking very much like Joker. It requires actual makeup remover to get off. And I basically had to take off all my makeup because it just runs from your lips when you try to get it off. It was a nightmare. But it looked really dark and vivid until, you know, I had to use my mouth and sent it smearing everywhere.
cot: 1. Clues: 'so surprised at how much I dislike', 'really hate this formula', 'sticky', 'mess is completely unbearable', 'ends up all over your face', 'looking very much like Joker', requires makeup remover, had to remove all makeup. Initial positives: 'tons of color payoff', 'was pretty excited'. 2. Reasoning: Despite promising initial application and color quality, the wear experience is catastrophic—sticky texture, uncontrolled spreading, difficult removal, ruining overall makeup. The dramatic negative outcome ('Joker' appearance) overwhelms the initial positives. The 2-star rating acknowledges some merit but strong overall dissatisfaction. 3. Verdict: Negative sentiment due to severe usability failure.

text:
Title: Epilady 4 Legend
Review: Ok... I have little hair on my legs and... it's blonde.  So there's that.  But I LOVE this thing.  It pulls everything I want it to.  And then I'm good for several weeks.  It's not a clean, close shave like a razor.  It doesn't "cut" hair - it pulls it out.  I recommend using after a nice hot bath or shower.  Opens pores and it's less painful - but honestly, it doesn't hurt that much - and I'm a baby!  This one is a replacement for an older model that finally gave out and wouldn't charge any more - after about 8 years or so of use.  This one is a charger unit - so you plug it into charger, fully charge and you'll get around 30- 40 minutes of work.  I find it's enough to do both legs (calves, ankles, knees - I don't usually do my thighs) I don't recommend for underarms as underarm skin is too delicate and might get caught up in the device.  I suppose if you can hold your underarm skin really taunt you MIGHT be able to.  I think it's better to use a razor there or other hair remover cream - just my opinion.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.
cot: 1. Clues: 'LOVE this thing', 'pulls everything I want it to', 'good for several weeks', 'doesn't hurt that much', 'replacement for an older model that finally gave out after about 8 years', strong recommendation with usage tips. 2. Reasoning: Despite minor caveats about hair type and incomplete sentence at end, the reviewer expresses enthusiastic satisfaction, long-term loyalty (8-year previous use), and detailed positive usage experience. The emotional language ('LOVE') and practical endorsement clearly indicate strong positive sentiment. 3. Verdict: Strongly positive sentiment matching 5-star rating.

I do, however, feel a difference in the elasticity of my skin and there is a noticeable glow. Can't recommend this 100%, but it might work for you, so worth a try (even just for the improved moisturized and softness of your skin).

text:
Title: Wow
Review: I got the color red hot. It was dark inside where the venue was so the color looks a smidge darker but it is a red with blue undertones. It reminds me of The Lip Bar’s Boy Trouble. If you want more of a cherry red, get Wicked. But I Loved this lipstick. It has nice slip when applied and stays like a dream!!  I had bbq ribs, collared greens and a drink in that picture and the color barely budged. There was zero transfer on my glass or fork or my teeth and still looked great for pictures. The longevity is astounding! It’s a dupe for Urban Decay Vice Lip Bond. I found it sticky the most when I applied a balm underneath it. Just apply one layer alone, and let it dry. The little stickiness after that felt more moisturizing than anything.  I came home, showered, washed off all of my makeup and it was still on!! U will Definitely need some makeup remover to remove efficiently. I used Vaseline because that’s all I had and it worked with some elbow grease. Anyway, I hope they come out with more colors! Definitely Buy These!!  And Stock Up!!
cot: 1. Clues: Triple repetition of 'Love', superlative ('my favorite'), universal approval ('all good'), functional benefit ('soft without feeling greasy'). 2. Reasoning: Extreme conciseness with maximum positive intensity. The repetition emphasizes genuine enthusiasm beyond casual satisfaction. Specific texture preference (non-greasy softness) indicates informed evaluation. No qualifications or suggestions for improvement. 3. Verdict: Strongly positive sentiment.

Title: darker colors are better for me Review: I wanted to try this new moisture stick and liked that the color was light. I will say that the "darker" cherry me is a bit better color. Honestly, these sticks remind me a little of those "tinker bell" lip glosses you used to get when you were a preteen. :) That probably dates me. :) I just got them and I have to say I prefer the darker colors because the tint is a bit better for me, adding a bit of actual color. I don't think the moisture lasts "8 hours" as advertised, but I don't mind putting it on. The "peach" has the least amount of scent to me and it's not quite "peach"... it smells.. sweet.
Hope this helps!

The current price of $15.83 comes to a slightly pricey $1.58 per tube.

text:
Title: Amazingly softer, smoother and healthy skin after 2 weeks of daily use!
Review: I am a 62 year old woman who has been trying a variety of creams and lotions to help diminish the signs of aging on my hands to no avail. Although I have only been using this daily for a little over 2 weeks, I an honestly say the skin on my hands is visibly plumper and smoother (and definitely a lot healthier looking). This Pre de Provence product has a terrifically light citrus fragrance, goes on full but absorbs quickly into the skin (not sure if it's because my skin is so dry it 'drinks' it up readily) and overall is a great product to use. The argan oil assures that your skin will be soft and smooth. I love someone's review on here that talks about the price of this product. Honestly, if $15 is too much to keep your skin looking youthful and healthy, then I guess you shouldn't be buying any skin products. This Pre de Provence is a great product and a little goes a long way.
cot: 1. Clues: 'Amazingly softer', 'visibly plumper and smoother', 'terrifically light citrus fragrance', 'great product', 'love' - overwhelmingly positive descriptors. 2. Reasoning: The reviewer expresses genuine satisfaction with visible results, pleasant fragrance, and texture. The detailed personal experience and comparison to failed alternatives strengthen confidence. The truncated ending about price defense suggests continued enthusiasm. 3. Verdict: Strongly positive sentiment matching 5-star rating.

text:
Title: Fragrance is overwhelming and chemical to my nose Review: There are three 3.25 ounce tubes in this package.

The balm goes on easily and melts (as can be seen from the picture) into the skin from the heat of one's body. I applied this both in the evening after my shower and in the morning after another shower.  This product actually kept me from sleeping well the entire night because the fragrance is incredibly overwhelming and it is also chemical smelling (almost like Raid insect spray) to my nose.  I love fragrance and always feel the more overpowering the better, but not in this case.

This deodorant "protects" as a result of the intense fragrance.  I doubt body odor sweat can overpower this aroma. I personally never get results from any product except those that contain 17% or more of aluminum. Nevertheless, I can attest that it produces results and a "scent" headache in my case.

This is not for me. The price of $21.98 is competitive for three. Since a lot is dispensed as it easily melts on during application, it will probably not last as long as other deodorant.
cot: 1. Clues: 'goes on easily', 'melts into the skin' vs 'kept me from sleeping well', 'incredibly overwhelming', 'chemical smelling', 'almost like Raid insect spray', 'but not in this case'. 2. Reasoning: Functional positives exist but are obliterated by severe sensory harm. Sleep disruption is serious quality-of-life impact. The reviewer explicitly loves strong fragrances normally, making this negative more credible—it's not preference mismatch but genuine unpleasantness. The 'protects' is sarcastic-quoted. 3. Verdict: Negative sentiment matches 2-star rating, with slight mitigation from texture positives.

There is one real poor design feature that we were surprised to see it on these modern days of DVD players: the wireless dongle sticks out at the front of the player and it really looks poor aesthetically and we wished it was shorter or have it at the back of the player?

Title: Runs large but seems well made Review: I have a 24" waist, ordered an xxs. They are big but i can make them work with the included belt. They are warm and seem well constructed. Due to being so big be they make a weird noise when I walk and the legs rub together. I bought these for field work on cold, damp days. I wish they sold smaller sizes. Calling pants with a 28" waist extra extra small is a little ridiculous.

I wore these into a pool of water while doing fieldwork in 40F weather. The water rolled right off the outer layer, the fleece got wet (obviously, these are pants not waders) but stayed warm and dried fast. I'm really glad to have these and I will be wearing them on future expeditions. I think they would also be good for hiking in wet, cold weather.
cot: 1. Clues: 'big', 'weird noise', 'legs rub together', 'ridiculous' sizing (negative); 'warm', 'well constructed', 'water rolled right off', 'stayed warm', 'dried fast', 'really glad', 'will be wearing on future expeditions' (strong positive). 2. text:
Title: Great Wireless...  Not 10/100/1000 speeds for wired.
Review: As a wireless router... top notch for today's current standards in the home with good range. I did not think to keep all the boxes for a return or I would.  I setup the wireless and was thrilled, I just assumed all was right in the world, so I tossed all the boxed.  Days later I realized my internal LAN speeds were not up to par with my cat 6 wiring.  Well, it is still new, but eBay here is comes.

Title: Convenient warming mask but a washcloth w/warm water is so much better Review: This is like a hand warmer for the eyes. There is no steam and not much value to this in my opinion. The only value is the convenience of an eye-warming mask when no warm water washcloth is available.

As per the instructions, I opened the mask which causes it to heat up. I then hooked the ear holes around my ears and laid down for 20 minutes with this on my eyes. It should not be pressed to the eyes. After 20 minutes, I removed it. Note the mask still remained warm for about 5 more minutes.  Upon removal, my eyes were lightly burning as if I got soap in my eyes. My lids felt moist but I would not call it steam. I personally feel a washcloth soaked in very warm water and placed on the eyelids is a much better solution.

This mask contains iron powder, activitate carbon, and water.

In summary, this product's only redeeming value is the convenience of having a warming mask when there is no warm water or washcloth available.

text:
Title: No-frills straightener with temp from 250 to 500 degrees; I am unsure when it reaches temp Review: The current price of $18.99 ($28.99 minus $10 coupon) is reasonable for this flat iron.

The iron's controls are on the interior of the blades, so it can be difficult to adjust. However, many flat irons have this feature. There is a wheel temperature dial that goes from 250 to 500 degrees Fahrenheit but there is no LED readout. This means I never know when it reaches that temperature because the red light stays on all the time when the unit is turned on.

The cord has a 360 swivel to prevent tangling, which I love. Also, the alignment of the plates is spot on--not too tight or too loose.

I used it to straighten my hair and it did an okay job. I had to do a lot of passes because the plate is only 3.5" long.

In summary, this is an okay one inch flat iron. The iron itself works but it is basic and I do not like that I cannot tell when the plates have heated up to the set temperature. The results are satisfactory but nothing outstanding. The iron's best feature is its swivel cord. It also comes with a 1-year warranty.
cot: 1. Clues: 'reasonable', 'love' the swivel, 'spot on' alignment vs 'difficult to adjust', 'never know when it reaches that temperature', 'did an okay job'. 2. Reasoning: The 3-star rating reflects a functional but frustrating product with significant usability issues (temperature uncertainty) and mediocre performance. Balanced mix of likes and dislikes. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment matching the 3-star rating.

Title: 4X fits like a 2x, and no room for a big butt Review: So I had really high hopes for these leggings. Ive moved to a much colder, northern state after ten years in the south US, and wanted some nice leggings. Mine were falling apart anyway, so why not these. Im usually a 3X, sometimes a 4X depending on the brand, so I went with the 4X. If they were too big, I thought I'd throw them in the dryer.

I was SO excited to try them on, but when i pulled them on, they barely went past my thighs. Half my backside was hanging out, and I still had five inches of legging hanging off my feet. I thought "well they must be caught on something" Nope. "They must be the wrong size..." Nope! All the tags, stickers and labels said 4X.

Long story short, Im returning them. If youre a size 24 or 26, and/or have a rather full butt, give these a pass. Two stars because like... the material is great. Its super soft. But god... I dont know on what planet a 4X fits like a 2X.
cot: 1. Clues: 'really high hopes,' 'SO excited' (strong positive anticipation); 'but' (pivot), 'barely went past my thighs,' 'Half my backside was hanging out,' 'returning them.' 2. Reasoning: Extensive narrative builds emotional investment then crushes it with complete sizing failure. The 4X fitting like 2X is a drastic mislabeling. The detailed struggle description emphasizes frustration. No positive qualities found in actual product. 3. Verdict: Strongly negative due to critical sizing error.

I like that feature but others might not want something with no label on it.

In summary, this is nice lip balm but way too pricey.

Title: Manicure bench is narrow, tall, slippery, & it smells Review: At the current price of $17.29, this manicure bench is more reasonably priced than a competitor with the same bench. The quality of the workmanship is pretty good but the legs elevate this bench about 5.25" off the ground. Since the bench is narrow on top, the person leaning their hands may have a tendency to tip this over. In addition, there are no rubber feet on the "legs." As a result, it is necessary to put a towel under this bench to keep it from slipping when placed on a solid non-stick surface.

Other than this, this bench is very nice. If it were lower to the ground, about two inches wider, and had rubberized feet on the bottom, this would have received 5 stars. In its current state it is functional but not the best it could be. The fact that it smells is also bothersome to me. I suppose it could double as a child's pretend ironing board.

No more restarting my router every other day to get it to work, and NO MORE NETGEAR FOR ME! There's a reason I guess why their routers are less expensive. Do yourself a favor and pay the extra ten dollars for another brand like Linksys.

If you do buy this one note that when I followed the instructions on the included setup CD it didn't work. When I just did the setup manually it worked fine. If you know anything at all about wireless routers then I suggest you set it up manually without the CD.

On the one hand that tiny size means you can install them just almost anywhere, but that also means there is almost no useable light from these. I wanted to use them in the halls just outside the guestroom doors to guide guests.

The light is very faint and barely lluminates the fixture, never mind where to step on the floor below. I moved them to the bathroom where the light can reflect in the big mirror and that's a definite improvement. I'm going to stick with the regular old plug in nightlights for inside the house and leave Mr Beams to do outdoor spotlights.

Title: Just what I was looking for. Review: I give it 5 stars with 2 caveats. Ultimately, I gave it 5 stars because a) i had just a few songs in the missing formats and b) once I got past the navigation issues, it works quite well and the company was very helpful when I needed them.

Update: Turns out as I loaded more music I found a lot more music that wasn't found while in BT, including some albums where some of the songs appeared and some didn't. Al in all, it's kind of clumsy to navigate in BT and BT was the feature I wanted most.

However, it sounds good with the earbuds that they provide and it's size makes it convenient. The 8gb are plenty if you don't have a lot of music. I do, so I added a 32gb sd mini-card which is as large as it will take.

Just be sure you remove it nightly and don't plan to wear it if you may be exposed to any moisture (working out, weather, etc).

Title: Cream packaging not in English; cream is hydrating but acts as barrier; aroma okay Review: This jar of face cream comes with several small samples of their other products. This is a disappointment.

I tried this on my face and neck and the cream is hydrating. I personally am not crazy about the subtle odor. It is just okay and I'm glad it is light. Also the cream stays on top of my skin like a barrier and does not absorb much. This face cream is just okay and is not something I would order again. It lacks the instructions, absorption, and wonderful fragrance I am looking for in a face cream. It does have the hydration effect it should, however. I woke up in the morning with my face feeling really moisturized.

text:
Title: Large barely fragrant bath bombs w/zero performance; turns water inky green Review: There are four individually wrapped bath bombs in this set. They are lightly fragrant but not really pleasingly so.

I dissolved one bomb in close to hot water and there were no suds created, only a fizzing action. The water immediately looked inky green. Since I personally do not like the color rings caused by bath bombs in my bath, I use them in a foot bath. One bath bomb dissolves in less than five minutes in close to hot water. The fragrance was barely detectable. I personally would never want to bathe with these. I am glad, however, that the color did not adhere to my skin, my foot bath, or my towels.

The current price of $10.99 comes to $2.75 per lackluster bath bomb. I'll pass on these as they are one of the worst bath bombs sets I ever tried.
cot: 1. Clues: 'barely fragrant', 'zero performance', 'inky green', 'lackluster', 'never want to bathe with these', 'barely detectable'. 2. Title: Great body balm, perfect for regular use!

I highly recommend this to everyone and the fragrance is light and great smelling as well!

text:
Title: Absolutely love my new Kindle HD!
Review: I was currently an Ipad 4 user, having owned an Ipad 3 previously as well. I thought I was sold on Apple until till I purchased my nephew a 7" Kindle Fire HD for Christmas. When he opened the box and began to use it, I was mesmorized with the quality of the picture and the amazing sound. Being that I have been a Prime member for years, I began to consider purchasing one for myself so I could benefit from the free Kindle book loans and streaming videos. I purchased this 4G LTE 8.9" version on the Kindle Fire HD and after having used it for several days now non-stop, I assure you that the Ipad is going into the closet (or will be made a gift to one of my neices shortly). I could not resist the $49.99 package for 250 mb a month for 12 months along with the $50 credit Amazon was doing as a promotion. Surfing the web is easy, speed is amazing, I am just so happy with my purchase. You honestly can't get a better deal out there. I love the Fire HD!

After using this, I noticed no difference in my face or neck compared to the creams and oils I use twice per day that are about $60 cheaper. In fact, my face looks a bit wind burned after using this and feels on the dry side. This is not an oil I would order again.

Fortunately, I don’t have to carry my IPad around like  some people do for their job.  I use mine mostly at a table or desk.  I’m happy that this cover has 3 grooves (instead of the 2 that many covers offer) to hold the IPad at different angles while being worked on, but it would be nice if they were deeper because when working on the IPad when it’s in the vertical position, it sometimes slides out of the groove and slams down.

text:
Title: Perfect mount for my Bose soundbar
Review: I will start by saying I have an Omnimount tv mount and when I saw the sound bar mount in Vine, I was very excited to say the least. I have no idea if this shampoo will stop hair loss but the fact that it cannot be used daily worries me as it is probably more of a medical product than a general shampoo. I hate the aroma and how it makes my hair feel.

In order to get these back in the case, the lever on the files have to be flipped into the low-profile position - meaning toward the handle.

The current price of $16.95 is competitive for the set picture but too pricey for the set received. I would not expect to pay more than $12 for the set received. Nevertheless, this is a very nice set that works as designed.

The fact that it even lasted eight hours is amazing because other non-aluminum deodorants last me only two to four hours and one lasted me six hours. Since I take a shower every 12 hours, I do not need long-length protection. 

The results are four stars for me considering my track record with non-aluminum deodorants.. I rated it two stars because I absolutely hate the rosemary witch hazel scent. I cannot even describe the aroma, but it offends my senses. I am glad my nose is not near my armpit because I would have thrown this out already if it were.

The current price of $17.99 is expensive for this 2.8-ounce tube.

Title: Basic trimmer but am not crazy about the “play” in the ON/OFF button Review: [[VIDEOID:9d67c538f01da31ac9da512b473049d9]] This is a very basic trimmer that works as designed. It comes with a carry case, a base, USB charging cable (no A/C adapter), cleaning brush, the shaver unit, and a guide comb.

I charged this about two hours before use until it said 100%. It can be charged either on or off the base.  I then tried this both with and without the guide comb. I like using it on my boyfriend's sideburns without the comb. It gives a good shave then.

There are two things I do not like. First, I do not like the power button. Every time I press it, the case cover bends in. Also, it is tricky to use. I have to press hold to turn on the light. If I don't press hold again, then the light stays on after I turn off the unit. I just find ON/OFF operations tricky and something I have to get used to. In addition, I found the razor itself hard to reseat after cleaning it. Other than this, it works as designed.

The current price of $21.59 ($26.99 minus 20%) is competitively priced for this.
cot: 1. Clues: 'works as designed', 'gives a good shave' vs 'very basic', 'not crazy about', power button issues, 'tricky to use'. 2. Reasoning: Functional adequacy with notable usability frustrations. The product performs its core task but with annoying design flaws. Verdict: Neutral—functional but flawed.

To my surprise, this works great! I do my nails myself and use this gel dryer and it is just like being in the salon (minus the salon germs, lol). I still will probably go to the salon every 4-6 weeks or so because I do like the way the salon works my cuticles. However, I feel I am easily saving $25 a month now doing every other application from the convenience of my own home. Highly recommend!

Title: Burns
Review: I am in my early 50's and regularly use anti-aging products. I don't often use acid peels, but I do exfoliate a few times per week. My skin can be sensitive to some products, but I have not figured out what specific ingredients trigger a reaction. Immediately upon wiping this product on my face, it started to painfully burn. I had to start rinsing it off right away. None of the other acid peels that I have used before have burned like this. It hurt! The burning sensation did subside after a few minutes. I was afraid to continue with my usual skincare routine and opted to just apply a mild moisturizer and call it a day.  I was surprised that there was no safety seal on the jar. That seems like a pretty standard thing these days.
cot: 1. Clues: The single-word title 'Burns' sets an alarming tone. The review describes immediate painful burning requiring emergency rinsing, explicit pain ('It hurt!'), and fear that disrupted their entire skincare routine. The safety seal absence adds quality concerns. The only mitigation is that pain subsided and they have sensitive skin. 2. Reasoning: Physical pain and safety concerns are among the most severe negative experiences in product reviews. The reviewer emphasizes this was uniquely bad compared to similar products. The 2-star rating suggests the product isn't completely non-functional (they acknowledge it may work for others), but the personal experience was traumatic enough to warrant strong dissatisfaction. The missing safety seal compounds quality concerns. 3. Verdict: Negative sentiment with slight acknowledgment that the product might work for non-sensitive skin types.

text:
Title: Alexa - "I can't do that from Fire TV" Garbage Product
Review: Maybe it's just me as an older guy, but I really wasn't expecting this. Unlike ANY remote I've ever used. NO control over TV volume, mute, 'back', numbers for channel, etc. Nope, this thing (streaming) is NOT intuitive to use, freezes, pixelates, is slow to change channels, and is a pain to use. "Alexa" has more of "I cannot do that for_____ reason" than it has use. Alexa listens to me about as well as an old deaf dog! Useless 80% of the time. If I'd have known all this I would have probably gone with an antenna and free, over-the-air TV viewing. I hate this thing and "help" is a matter of luck with no humans involved. I have to have my 16 y.o. grandson help with some things. Get ready to carry a bunch of remotes and such (Fire control, TV remote and cordless phone if you carry that around). I really, really don't like this POS!
cot: 1. Clues: 'Garbage Product', 'NOT intuitive', 'freezes', 'pixelates', 'slow', 'pain to use', 'Useless 80% of the time', 'I hate this thing' - intensely negative throughout. 2. Reasoning: The review expresses comprehensive frustration across multiple dimensions: hardware (remote), software (freezing, pixelation), voice assistant functionality, and customer support. The 'hate' declaration and regret of purchase decision indicate strong negative sentiment. The 2-star rating (not 1-star) may reflect that the product technically functions or the reviewer self-blames ('Maybe it's just me'), but the experience is clearly very poor. 3. Title: Good for an hour/connection not consistent Review: When connected to the USB-C port of a Dell XPS 13 laptop the screen worked as expected. It would come up as mirrored when first connect and would need to be changed to extended. The screen quality looked quite similar to the screen on the XPS 13. Connecting to a 15” MacBook Pro was a different story.

It was hit or miss when connecting to the MacBook Pro. Sometimes it would work an other times it would say no signal. When it did work there was a noticeable. The Retina Display is significantly sharper than the Acer External display. If you don’t mind that, it is nice having a second display for your MacBook Pro.

The construction of the external display is a bit cheap. In certain areas the bezel does not make contact with the screen.

You must plug your laptop into a power source or the external display will DRAIN YOUR BATTERY in short order. You may get 1 hour of use.

It’s on the inexpensive side so it is worth a try.
cot: 1. Clues: 'worked as expected', 'quite similar', 'nice having' (positives) vs 'not consistent', 'hit or miss', 'no signal', 'noticeable' [quality difference], 'bit cheap' (negatives). The reviewer acknowledges both scenarios fairly, noting conditional value ('If you don't mind that'). 'Cheap' construction criticism adds to negative side. The overall assessment is that it's usable but flawed, with significant compatibility and quality limitations. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with balanced recognition of pros and cons.

When using the Remington flat iron keep your hands on the lower top that does not get hot. As it gets hot above and below
The plates on the outside.

Hair type and dial.
Fine or easy to style 1-9.

Textured 16-24.
Thick or hard to style 25-30.

text:
Title: weather station Review: UPDATE BELOW I bought this to replace one that was 6 years old... the sensor bar quit transmitting. They don't last forever and we live on the oceanfront - which takes a toll. This one is a newer, fancier version - no more transmitter bar - it's integrated into the main outdoor unit. There is a strip at the bottom of the indoor reader unit that is constantly streaming facts of your weather... lowest temp with date, highest temp with date, rain totals.. etc... it's just streams at the bottom of the unit. You can control how fast or slow it streams. The only thing that I don't like is that the barometer portion is small and small to read numbers. My old one had an easy to read bar graph. I kinda miss that part - but honestly this is a nice unit. So far so good - however I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.
11/7/2020... This unit is only 9 months old and just quit. VERY disappointed to say the least. It started registering 50 BELOW zero at about 45 degrees. Change all batteries... cleaned all sensors... no way to fix it. Just junk. Dang it.
cot: 1. Clues: The review contains multiple positive elements: 'newer, fancier version,' 'nice unit,' appreciation for streaming weather facts, adjustable speed. The rating-text contradiction requires acknowledging the rating while recognizing the text suggests the user may have intended a higher rating or made an error. Title: Surface edge is too small & does not really flatten my hair; also the buzzing noise bothers me Review: I love the purple color of this iron and how good it feels in my hands. The swivel action of the cord is also nice. The heat dial goes from 280 to 430 degrees and is on the side of the iron next to the on/off button.

The heat settings recommended are:
- 280 to 330 for fine, thin hair
- 330 to 380 for wavy, medium-textured hair
- 380 to 430 for thick, coarse hair

An insert in the package says that the unit will emit smoke and an odor initially due to the oil-based protective coating which will burn off. I detected neither issue.

However, there are several things I do not like about this iron. First, the one-inch wide plate is only 3-1/2 inches long. I would have preferred a little bit longer heating plate. Second, no matter how high I put the heat setting, it barely did anything for my thin, fine hair. Third, I really cannot stand the electrical noise of the iron. There's this buzzing sound during operation that scares me. For all these reasons, I will never use this iron again.
cot: 1. Clues: 'love the purple color', 'good it feels in my hands', 'swivel action is also nice', but also 'does not really flatten my hair', 'buzzing noise bothers me', 'several things I do not like'. Verdict: Negative sentiment with acknowledged design positives.

text:
Title: One end is mascara, other end is fibers to make lashes longer. Review: Interesting concept for longer looking lashes.
One side of the tube is white lint-fibers, and the other side is a black mascara.

First you put on the mascara, curl your lashes, apply the fibers, and more mascara,Go back and forth
With this prodedure till you get the look you want.
Your lashes do look longer.
I applied this two times. I don't wear heavy mascara or makeup.

That said, the mascara is not water proof. At first I got some of the fiber in my eye. I had to pick a little
Off the end of the wand, then apply it.
For me I found the mascara abit messy.. it might of been it was new, and a different brand then I usually use!
At night before washing this off, I rubbed my eyes, and the mascara came off in little flakes. I'm thinking,
With the mascara comes the fibers and leaves a little mess on my eyes, and face.

3 stars
cot: 1. Clues: 'Interesting concept', 'Your lashes do look longer' (positive outcome); 'not water proof', 'fiber in my eye', 'abit messy', 'different brand then I usually use' (negatives). 2. Reasoning: Concept validation and achieved effect balanced against application difficulties, safety concern, and user adaptation needs. The 'That said' pivot and tentative attribution ('might of been') suggest incomplete resolution. The 3-star rating reflects this mixed experience with learning curve. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with moderate confidence.

I had no coverage over any of my gray hairs, nothing helped with the thin brows, I didn't even notice a color on the brows I did have covered. I got the light brown color which should have been fine for my coloring, but maybe one needs a dark color to make it noticeable? Anyway, I would never order this again. As an fyi, I usually like Dermablend products, this one is not up to their usual standards.

I'm sticking with less expensive watches now so it doesn't hurt so much if it turns out the item is a DUD.  Anyway, happy birthday to me - not!

text:
Title: So-so in the tropics Review: I'm beginning to believe that there's no anti-fog product that will work when you're living on the edge of the Amazon rainforest. Based on the reviews, this one seemed to be my best bet to prevent my spectacles and camera lens from fogging when leaving an air-conditioned area for the hot and humid outside air.

Unfortunately, it does not work at all for spectacles and with limited success on the camera lens. However, I will continue to use this product until something better comes along, as instead of the whole lens fogging up after being treated, there were only random patches of fog on the camera lens, and these cleared up a lot faster than with the untreated lens. (I gave up on the spectacle lenses)

If anyone can suggest what I may be doing wrong, I'll be more than pleased to revisit this review through fog-free lenses.

Amanda Richards, December 15, 2011
cot: 1. Clues: 'So-so' title, 'does not work at all', 'limited success', 'Unfortunately'; 'However', 'continue to use', 'cleared up a lot faster than untreated'. 2. Reasoning: Failed primary use case (spectacles) but partial success on secondary (camera) with measurable improvement. Continued use despite failure indicates some value. Extreme environment acknowledged as challenging. Balanced, analytical tone with scientific observation ('random patches'). 3-star rating reflects 'partial success in difficult conditions'. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with balanced assessment of failure and partial benefit.
